page: Microsoft Azure FX8mds_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the FXmdsv2 family. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical Intel Xeon 8573C cores running at a base clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The server is configured with 168.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 21.0 GB per core. It includes 472 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-threaded capabilities, with Geekbench single-core scores and single-threaded gzip compression rates reaching top-tier performance levels. Geekbench text processing also ranks in the top tier. However, multi-core performance metrics, such as stress-ng multi-core CPU and Geekbench multi-core scores, fall into the average range. Memory bandwidth tests show an average read-only small block speed of 691.35 GB/sec, while uncached large block read and write speeds are 66.42 GB/sec and 45.83 GB/sec respectively.

The hardware profile presents a clear tradeoff: while single-core execution and memory capacity are exceptionally high, the physical core count is limited to four, resulting in average multi-threaded scaling. For instance, LLM inference text generation is top-tier for small 135M models at 344.1 tokens/sec but drops to a weak rating for medium 7B models at 8.5 tokens/sec. This server is suited for memory-intensive workloads that do not require massive parallelization, such as relational databases, in-memory caching, text processing, and single-threaded data compression tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure FX8mds_v2 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores, 168.0 GB of system memory, and 472 GB of local storage. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance delivers top-tier single-core performance, as evidenced by its Geekbench single-core and single-threaded gzip compression benchmarks. However, its multi-core performance remains average due to the physical core limitation. The high memory-to-core ratio of 21.0 GB per core makes it highly resource-dense for memory-bound tasks, though it exhibits a performance tradeoff in highly parallelized workloads like medium-scale LLM inference. It is best suited for memory-intensive databases, text processing, and single-threaded operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX8mds_v2 is a memory-optimized server with 8 vCPUs, 168 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX8mds_v2 is an 8-vCPU memory-optimized server with 168 GB RAM and 472 GB local storage, running on a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU.
tagline: A memory-intensive server featuring high clock-speed single-core performance, substantial RAM density, and dedicated local storage for memory-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs on 4 physical Intel Xeon 8573C cores running at 4.2 GHz
168.0 GB of system memory providing 21.0 GB per core
472 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and text processing benchmarks
Average multi-core scaling due to physical core constraints
Suited for memory-intensive databases, caching, and single-threaded tasks
